note: Content pack for hksaperstein.github.io site agent. Captions are first-person-adaptable facts; narrative copy in content/*.md is already in VOICE.md register. Numbers all trace to repo sources (public: github.com/hksaperstein/rl, branch franka-panda-pivot).

videos.caption: Commanded d20 on a five-die table: detector finds it, depth deprojection gives the 3D target, staged IK descends, grasps, lifts. Pass, z-gain 237.1mm.
Commanded d10, one of the two smallest dice. The trial where grasp-position tolerance mattered: 15mm tolerance closed beside the die; ~5mm fixed it. Pass, z-gain 239.3mm.

Commanded d4. Detection and positioning converge to sub-millimeter accuracy and the pick still fails: flat parallel pads squeeze the tetrahedron out (z-gain ~0.3mm vs 237-241mm on the four that succeed). Declared a permitted failure before the run; it failed exactly that way. Open grasp-strategy problem, not a bug.
RL policy trained 1500 iters on the real-size (30.3mm) d20: reaches, never grasps. 0/8 sustained lifts - the falsified arm of the joint-space experiment.
First learned die lift on this platform: d20 scaled to 48mm, 8/8 sustained lifts (asset-bisect rung 2, seed 123).
Same training recipe, DexCube object: decisive lift+carry (8/8 sustained, position_error 0.105 vs 0.216 do-nothing baseline). Proves the recipe; isolates the failure to the die asset.

images.caption: Real d10 photo, synthetic-only v1 detector: mislabeled d12 (the apparent-size confound)
images.provenance: vision/models/eval overlays, frozen real test set

images.caption: Same class of photo after the close-up training slice: correct d10 at high confidence
images.provenance: vision/models/eval overlays, frozen real test set

stats.label: die types picked correctly by the vision-driven grasp (seed 42); the d4 is the open failure

real-photo detection mAP50 before/after fixing the apparent-size confound in synthetic training data; both pre-registered thresholds (>=0.40) passed. d6 regressed 0.519 -> 0.275 (root-caused: d10 glyph-convention attractor)
envs with sustained lift on the first RL checkpoint that ever lifted a die (48mm d20, asset-bisect rung 2 seed 123)
mixed-size curriculum falsified same-day: size-DR dilutes discovery when discovery is the bottleneck; staged-anneal fallback is the next arm
cost of the first full cloud training run (GCP spot L4): create, install Isaac Sim, train 1200 iters, sync checkpoints, tear down
